build_from.reason: ResolveExecBackend (internal/verify/exec.go:24-57) already implements the exact pattern this epic needs: given a *registry.SandboxConfig, build a sandbox.DefaultDockerConfig(), override it from the config block, construct a sandbox.NewDockerBackend, and run Preflight before returning a ready sandbox.Backend. It was built for the `--exec` reviewer-reproduction feature (Epic 11.0) but the resolve-and-preflight shape is identical to what auto-fix's validation step needs.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add a sibling resolver (or generalize ResolveExecBackend) that autofix.go's validateAutoFixBackend gate calls to obtain a sandbox.Backend for the post-apply validation step, defaulting to sandboxed execution and refusing (or falling back, per the --no-sandbox flag) when no backend is configured/available. Route internal/verify.RunConfiguredValidation's command execution through sandbox.Backend.Run(ctx, sandbox.RunSpec{Command: argv, SnapshotDir: applyTarget}) instead of exec.CommandContext directly, when sandboxing is enabled. Verified plumbing facts: validateAutoFixBackend already receives proj *registry.ProjectConfig (called with cfg.Project at cmd/atcr/review.go:353), so proj.Sandbox (internal/registry/project.go:85) is already in scope at the gate — no new config plumbing is needed. RunConfiguredValidation has exactly one production caller (cmd/atcr/autofix.go:252), so a signature change or sibling-function split touches a single call site.

existing_patterns.name: Opt-in execution gate with refuse-without-backend
existing_patterns.description: internal/verify.ResolveExecBackend(ctx, execEnabled, sc) returns (nil,nil,0,nil) when execEnabled is false, and hard-errors via ErrExecNoBackend when true but sc is nil, requiring Preflight() to pass before returning a ready sandbox.Backend. cmd/atcr's `--exec` flag on verify/review wires this via resolveExec in cmd/atcr/verify.go:46 (call at :54).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Building the analogous resolver for --auto-fix's validation step; also the model for how a --no-sandbox opt-out flag should invert the default (sandbox on by default, explicit flag to bypass, with a security warning) rather than mirroring --exec's off-by-default posture.

existing_patterns.name: Backend interface + DockerBackend implementation
existing_patterns.description: internal/sandbox.Backend is a 3-method interface (Name, Preflight, Run) with RunSpec (Command []string XOR Script string, Timeout, SnapshotDir) and RunResult (Command, ExitCode, Output, TimedOut). internal/sandbox.DockerBackend (internal/sandbox/docker.go) is the sole implementation: network-isolated, read-only snapshot mount, resource-capped, non-root ephemeral container. Verified mount/env details (dockerRunArgs, docker.go:103-143): SnapshotDir is mounted `-v <dir>:/work:ro` with `--workdir /work`; a writable tmpfs is mounted at /scratch; HOME, TMPDIR, XDG_CACHE_HOME, GOCACHE, and GOTMPDIR are all redirected to /scratch (docker.go:127-131) — so Go build/test commands needing a build cache already work against a read-only tree. Run treats docker runtime exit codes 125-127 and signal deaths (128+) as backend FAULTS (Go errors), not program results, and reports timeout as TimedOut with the conventional exit code 124.
existing_patterns.follow_for: This IS the sandbox to wire into autofix — no new sandbox package/backend is needed (confirmed by the epic's own /refine-epic redundant-work correction).

existing_patterns.name: All-or-nothing CLI gate collecting every missing piece
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/autofix.go:validateAutoFixBackend (:107) resolves apply target, validation command/timeout, and GitHub credentials in one pass, appending every problem to a `missing []string` and returning a single usageError naming all of them, rather than failing on the first missing piece.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Adding sandbox backend resolution (and the --no-sandbox override) as a fourth piece in this same gate, consistent with the existing fail-fast, name-everything-missing style.

existing_patterns.name: Config-block validation with Validate() on the registry type
existing_patterns.description: internal/registry/sandbox.go:SandboxConfig.Validate() checks backend name, required image/test_command, and shape-validates memory/cpus/timeout strings — called at config load, not at execution time. internal/registry/autofix.go:AutoFixConfig.Validate() follows the same load-time pattern for the auto_fix block.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Whether auto-fix reuses the same `sandbox:` config block (extending SandboxConfig with fields auto-fix needs, e.g. a distinct image/test_command for validation) or needs a parallel block; needs a design decision, not assumed here. If a config-level opt-out (e.g. auto_fix.no_sandbox) is added, its shape-validation belongs in AutoFixConfig.Validate() at load time, mirroring both existing blocks.

existing_patterns.name: Security-relevant opt-out `--no-*` flag
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/review.go:87 registers `--no-ignore` (Bool, default false), which bypasses the .gitignore/.atcrignore payload filter — the closest existing precedent for a flag that relaxes a security boundary. Other `--no-*` precedents: --no-cache (review.go:86), --no-scorecard/--no-local-debt (reconcile.go:48-49). None of them prints a warning today; the stderr security-warning precedent is cmd/atcr/main.go:348 (unrecognized ATCR_TELEMETRY warning).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Registering --no-sandbox in addAutoFixFlags via cmd.Flags().Bool, and printing its security warning to stderr on EVERY --no-sandbox run (plan.md Risk Mitigation requires every-run, not once) — strictly louder than any existing opt-out flag.

related_files.purpose: RunConfiguredValidation (:80) is the current post-apply validation runner for --auto-fix (Sprint 17.0): it shells out directly on the host via os/exec.CommandContext with a process-group timeout kill. This is the exact call site that must be routed through internal/sandbox when sandboxing is enabled. Note the result-shape mismatch the sandboxed path must bridge: ValidationResult carries split Stdout/Stderr, per-stream truncation flags, Duration, TimedOut, and StartError, while sandbox.RunResult carries a single combined Output, TimedOut, and ExitCode — and docker runtime faults surface as Go errors, not exit codes.

CLI-level gate (validateAutoFixBackend :107) and orchestration (runAutoFix :239) for `--auto-fix`. runAutoFix calls verify.RunConfiguredValidation directly after ApplyPatch at :252 — the single production call site of the host-side runner. autoFixBackend (:59) is the resolved-config struct the new sandbox backend (and the no-sandbox decision) should ride on into runAutoFix.

Wires the auto-fix flow into `atcr review`: addAutoFixFlags at :92, validateAutoFixBackend(cmd, cfg.Project, ".") at :353 (proves proj.Sandbox is already in scope at the gate), orchestrateAutoFix at :661. The --no-ignore opt-out flag precedent lives at :87. No changes anticipated if the resolved backend rides the existing autoFixBackend struct, but this is the file to touch if the gate call sites need the new flag value.

AutoFixConfig — the `auto_fix:` config block (apply_target, validate_command, validate_timeout) with load-time Validate(). NOT previously captured. This is the natural home for a config-level sandbox opt-out (the epic allows "a --no-sandbox flag or config option") or a nested auto-fix sandbox block, if design chooses one; its Validate() is where any new field's shape-check belongs.
ResolveExecBackend (:24) is the existing sandbox-resolution pattern (Epic 11.0) for the unrelated `--exec` reviewer-reproduction feature; reusable shape for autofix's own resolver.

Defines Backend interface, RunSpec, RunResult — the contract autofix's validation call must target. RunSpec.validate() (:43) requires an absolute, colon-free SnapshotDir (mount-spec injection guard) — auto-fix's gate already resolves applyTarget to an absolute path, satisfying this for free.

DockerBackend implementation (Preflight :270, Run :154, dockerRunArgs :103). The snapshot mount `:ro` is hardcoded at :133 — the single line a writable-mount design would touch. GOCACHE/GOTMPDIR/HOME/TMPDIR redirection to /scratch (:127-131) already covers the Go build-cache concern, narrowing the read-only question to commands that write INTO the tree (coverage profiles, codegen, linter --fix).

SandboxConfig — the `sandbox:` config block Epic 11.0 introduced. Validate() requires Image AND TestCommand unconditionally; auto-fix has its own auto_fix.validate_command, so reusing the block unmodified forces operators to configure test_command even when only --auto-fix (never --exec) is used. Needs a decision on reuse vs. extension vs. a parallel block.

ProjectConfig carries both `Sandbox *SandboxConfig` (:85) and `AutoFix *AutoFixConfig` — the two config blocks this plan's resolver reads. Reference for how the blocks are overlaid/loaded; no changes anticipated.

Existing security-posture documentation for the `--exec` sandbox (network isolation, resource caps, preflight) and the ONLY doc that documents the `sandbox:` config block today. Template/precedent for the docs/ security warnings this epic's Acceptance Criteria require for --no-sandbox.

Tests for ResolveExecBackend (RefusesWithoutBackend, BuildsAndPreflights, DisabledIsNoOp, PreflightFailureRefuses) plus the fakeDocker shim (:15) that injects a fake docker binary via DockerConfig.DockerPath — the test-shape AND test-infra precedent for the new autofix sandbox resolver's tests.

Existing tests pinning the RunConfiguredValidation / ValidationResult contract (Passed(), TimedOut, StartError, truncation). The sandboxed execution path must preserve this contract or consciously extend it — these tests define what 'no behavior change' means for the host path.

TestDockerRunArgs_* tests assert the exact docker argv (hardening flags, mount spec, env redirects) without a daemon. If design adds a writable-mount mode for auto-fix, this is where the new mount spec gets pinned.
Existing gate/orchestration tests for validateAutoFixBackend and runAutoFix (call-recording fakes). The sandbox-resolution fourth piece and --no-sandbox behavior need coverage here in the same style.

semantic_matches.relevance: The resolved-config struct the gate returns; the natural carrier for the resolved sandbox.Backend (and the --no-sandbox decision) from validateAutoFixBackend into runAutoFix.

Pure argv builder where the read-only mount is hardcoded (:133) and the /scratch env redirects live (:127-131) — the exact site a writable mount-mode design would touch, asserted today by sandbox_test.go without a daemon.
Pins the writable-env (GOCACHE/GOTMPDIR/HOME/TMPDIR -> /scratch) behavior that makes read-only-mount Go validation viable; the test home for any new mount-mode assertion.
Existing load-time validation tests for the auto_fix block — the precedent for testing any new config-level sandbox opt-out field.

integration_gaps.gap: No sandbox resolver for the auto-fix validation step
integration_gaps.impact: RunConfiguredValidation is host-only (os/exec); nothing turns a *registry.SandboxConfig into a preflighted sandbox.Backend for --auto-fix, so the validation command runs with the atcr process's full privileges.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Create internal/verify/autofix_exec.go (or similar) mirroring ResolveExecBackend's config-to-DockerConfig translation and Preflight-before-return shape, wired into validateAutoFixBackend's all-or-nothing gate. Already tracked in files_to_create.

integration_gaps.gap: RunSpec has no mount-mode option (read-only is hardcoded)
integration_gaps.impact: Auto-fix validates an ALREADY-MUTATED working tree; if a configured validation command must write into the tree (coverage profiles, codegen, linter --fix), the read-only `-v <dir>:/work:ro` mount cannot express it.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Explicit /design-sprint decision. Default Go validation (`go build ./...`, `go test ./...`) works read-only because GOCACHE/GOTMPDIR/HOME/TMPDIR redirect to the /scratch tmpfs (docker.go:127-131); only add a RunSpec mount-mode field + dockerRunArgs parameterization if design proves an in-tree write need.

integration_gaps.gap: No adapter between sandbox.RunResult and verify.ValidationResult
integration_gaps.impact: RunResult carries combined stdout+stderr, TimedOut (exit 124), and reports docker runtime exits 125-127/signal deaths as Go errors; ValidationResult carries split Stdout/Stderr, per-stream truncation flags, Duration, and StartError. runAutoFix's failure branches (verr vs !Passed()) depend on that taxonomy.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Build a deliberate translation in the sandboxed validation path (sibling function or Backend-parameterized variant), preserving the host path's contract byte-for-byte.

integration_gaps.gap: Config surface mismatch for auto-fix sandboxing
integration_gaps.impact: SandboxConfig.Validate() requires test_command unconditionally (an --exec concept), while auto-fix already has its own auto_fix.validate_command; and there is no config-level counterpart to --no-sandbox (the epic allows 'flag or config option').
integration_gaps.recommendation: Design decision: reuse `sandbox:` with a relaxed Validate(), extend SandboxConfig, or add a nested auto_fix sandbox/opt-out block validated in AutoFixConfig.Validate().

integration_gaps.gap: No documentation home for the --auto-fix security posture
integration_gaps.impact: The auto_fix config block is entirely undocumented in docs/ and --auto-fix has no dedicated page; the AC-required --no-sandbox security warnings have no natural place to land.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Extend docs/execution.md (already documents the sandbox: block and --exec security posture) with an auto-fix section, or create docs/auto-fix.md cross-linking it.

integration_points.description: Add sandbox backend resolution (default enabled, refusing per --no-sandbox semantics) alongside the existing apply-target/validate-command/GitHub-credential resolution, collected into the same all-or-nothing `missing` gate. proj.Sandbox is already in scope (review.go:353 passes cfg.Project).

The call to verify.RunConfiguredValidation (:252) must become sandbox-routed when the resolved backend is present, passing the applied working tree as RunSpec.SnapshotDir (already absolute — satisfies RunSpec.validate) and be.validateArgv as RunSpec.Command. Thread the backend through the autoFixBackend struct (:59).

Either this function gains a sandbox.Backend parameter/variant, or a new sibling function (e.g. RunConfiguredValidationSandboxed) wraps sandbox.Backend.Run and is selected by the caller. Whichever shape is chosen must adapt sandbox.RunResult (combined Output, TimedOut, exit 124, docker faults as Go errors) back into the ValidationResult contract (split Stdout/Stderr, truncation flags, Duration, StartError) that runAutoFix's failure handling consumes.
Register the new --no-sandbox flag here alongside the existing --auto-fix/--repo/--token/--api-url flags, with help text carrying the security warning.

The read-only snapshot mount is hardcoded (`-v <dir>:/work:ro` at docker.go:133) and RunSpec has no mount-mode field. Auto-fix validates an ALREADY-MUTATED tree, so if design requires in-tree writes (coverage output, codegen), this pair is where a writable-mount mode would be added — the epic's central open design question. Note Go build/test cache needs are already covered by the /scratch env redirects, so plain `go build ./...` / `go test ./...` validate fine against the read-only mount.

reusable_components.name: sandbox.Backend (interface, RunSpec, RunResult)
reusable_components.usage_example: res, err := backend.Run(ctx, sandbox.RunSpec{Command: argv, SnapshotDir: applyTarget, Timeout: timeout}) — SnapshotDir must be absolute and colon-free (RunSpec.validate).

reusable_components.name: sandbox.DockerBackend
reusable_components.usage_example: backend := sandbox.NewDockerBackend(cfg); if err := backend.Preflight(ctx); err == nil { /* ready for Run */ } — cfg starts from sandbox.DefaultDockerConfig() with registry overrides.

reusable_components.name: verify.ResolveExecBackend pattern
reusable_components.usage_example: Mirror its config-to-DockerConfig translation and Preflight-before-return shape for a new autofix-specific resolver.

reusable_components.name: fakeDocker test shim
reusable_components.usage_example: Writes a POSIX shell script to a temp dir and injects it as DockerConfig.DockerPath, so resolver/preflight tests run hermetically without a docker daemon (skips on Windows). Reuse for the new autofix resolver tests.

architecture_notes: The sandbox execution primitive already exists end-to-end (Epic 11.0) and is battle-tested via the `--exec` feature; this epic's real work is purely an integration + policy change (default-on vs. default-off, and where the resolved backend plugs into the auto-fix validation call), not new sandbox infrastructure.

The read-only mount question is NARROWER than first framed: docker.go:127-131 already redirects HOME/TMPDIR/XDG_CACHE_HOME/GOCACHE/GOTMPDIR into the writable /scratch tmpfs, so the Go build-cache assumption in localvalidate.go carries over automatically and the default `go build ./...` validation works against a read-only /work. The genuine casualties are validation commands that write INTO the tree (e.g. `go test -coverprofile=...`, codegen, linter --fix modes). Whether auto-fix needs a writable RunSpec mount mode is still an explicit /design-sprint decision — but the default Go path does not.

Result-shape and error-taxonomy mismatch to bridge: sandbox.RunResult has a single combined Output (no stdout/stderr split), TimedOut with exit code 124, and reports docker runtime exit codes 125-127 / signal deaths as Go ERRORS; verify.ValidationResult has split Stdout/Stderr, per-stream truncation flags, Duration, TimedOut, and StartError. The sandboxed path needs a deliberate translation, and runAutoFix's existing failure branches (verr != nil vs !res.Passed()) must keep their meaning.

Timeout precedence to decide at design time: auto-fix's validation timeout (auto_fix.validate_timeout, default 2m via cmd/atcr/autofix.go:36) vs. the sandbox block's timeout_secs (default 60s via sandbox.DefaultDockerConfig). RunSpec.Timeout should carry the auto-fix value so sandboxing does not silently halve the operator's configured budget.

internal/registry.SandboxConfig currently requires both Image and TestCommand unconditionally; auto-fix already has its own auto_fix.validate_command, so reusing the `sandbox:` block unmodified forces operators to set test_command even when they never use --exec. Reuse-with-relaxed-Validate vs. extension vs. a parallel auto_fix sandbox sub-block is an open design decision; internal/registry/autofix.go is the home for any config-level opt-out field.
cmd/atcr/autofix.go's validateAutoFixBackend already has an established fail-fast, name-everything-missing pattern the sandbox gate should follow rather than introducing a different error style.

Docs gap beyond the AC: the `auto_fix:` config block is entirely undocumented in docs/ today (no mention of apply_target/validate_command/validate_timeout anywhere under docs/), and the `sandbox:` block is documented only in docs/execution.md. The --no-sandbox security-warning work is the natural moment to document both; docs/registry.md currently covers neither block and docs_audit_test.go does not check them.

files_to_modify.reason: Add sandbox backend resolution to the auto-fix gate, add the --no-sandbox flag (addAutoFixFlags), carry the resolved backend on autoFixBackend, and route the runAutoFix validation call (:252) through the sandbox backend by default.
Add a sandboxed execution path for the post-apply validation command (currently host-only via os/exec), including the RunResult→ValidationResult translation.
Possibly extend SandboxConfig (or relax its Validate() test_command requirement) to support auto-fix's validation use case distinctly from the `--exec` use case.

Add a config-level sandbox opt-out (e.g. auto_fix.no_sandbox) or nested sandbox sub-block, with load-time shape validation — only if design chooses a config counterpart to the --no-sandbox flag (the epic allows either).

Add or cross-reference security warnings for --no-sandbox, mirroring the existing --exec security-posture section. This is also the only doc covering the sandbox: block, making it the natural home for documenting the auto_fix sandbox posture (auto_fix is undocumented today).
Extend gate/orchestration tests for the sandbox-resolution fourth piece, the --no-sandbox bypass + warning, and the sandbox-routed validation path (mirroring the fakeDocker-based resolver tests).
CONDITIONAL: only if /design-sprint decides auto-fix needs a writable mount mode — RunSpec gains a mount-mode field. Skip entirely if the read-only + /scratch-env model is accepted.
CONDITIONAL: only paired with the RunSpec mount-mode change — dockerRunArgs (:133) parameterizes the `:ro` mount suffix. Skip if read-only is accepted.

files_to_create.purpose: New resolver mirroring internal/verify/exec.go's ResolveExecBackend, specialized for auto-fix's validation-sandbox resolution and --no-sandbox handling.
Resolver tests mirroring exec_test.go's shape (refuses-without-backend, builds-and-preflights via the fakeDocker shim, no-sandbox is a no-op).

OPTIONAL: if design prefers a dedicated auto-fix doc — --auto-fix currently has no dedicated page (only passing mentions in docs/ci-integration.md and docs/agentic-consumption.md); it would host the --no-sandbox security warning, the auto_fix config surface, and a cross-link to docs/execution.md's sandbox guarantees.

test_patterns.example_test: internal/verify/exec_test.go (resolver shape + fakeDocker shim); internal/sandbox/sandbox_test.go (dockerRunArgs argv assertions without a daemon); cmd/atcr/autofix_test.go (gate/orchestration with call-recording fakes)
